remember: Students will be able to recall and identify various types of MySQL functions, including built-in functions and user-defined functions.
Students will be able to remember the syntax and usage of MySQL aggregate functions, such as SUM, AVG, MAX, MIN, and COUNT.
Students will be able to recall the concept of grouping in MySQL and how it is used with GROUP BY clause.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of MySQL functions and their applications in data manipulation and analysis.
Students will be able to comprehend the difference between aggregate functions and non-aggregate functions in MySQL.
Students will be able to understand how grouping is used to perform operations on a set of rows that have the same values in one or more columns.
apply: Students will be able to apply MySQL functions to solve real-world problems, such as data filtering, aggregation, and sorting.
Students will be able to use GROUP BY clause with aggregate functions to perform grouping operations.
Students will be able to use HAVING clause to filter grouped results.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the results of grouping operations and identify patterns and trends in the data.
Students will be able to use MySQL functions to analyze and summarize data, such as calculating totals, averages, and counts.

ncert_objectives: Describe the concept of MySQL functions and their types.
Explain the syntax and usage of MySQL aggregate functions.
Explain the concept of grouping in MySQL and its applications.
board_exam_objectives: Define MySQL functions and their significance in database management.
Identify and use various MySQL aggregate functions, such as SUM, AVG, MAX, MIN, and COUNT.
Use GROUP BY clause with aggregate functions to perform grouping operations and filter results with HAVING clause.
